other_observers: Cole Wild; first spotted by Cole
specific_location: AT&T Tower just south of Lamar
description: the bird was a flycatcher with a body slightly smaller than a kingbird, but with a very long tail that gave it a larger overall look. The overall shape was slimmer than a kingbird, again accentuated by the long tail. The bird had a tail about as long as it's body.  The head of the bird was a very pale gray, slightly darker on the nape. The underparts were also extremely pale gray, almost white. There was the faintest hint of pale pink on the lower belly, almost not noticeable. The bill and eye were black; the bill was short and thin. The wings were black, with bold white edges on the secondaries and coverts. The upperside of the tail was black, the underside was white. The back was pale-medium gray.Behaviors: the bird was seen in some small trees adjacent to the road. For the entire observation it sat on branches near the top of the trees and did not fly.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
